title: CM inaugurates Telangana Public School in Rangareddy district
content: Telangana Govt committed to create infra for quality education and skilling to make youth job ready, says CM

title: Fire breaks out at private children’s hospital in Vijayawada; all patients safe
content: Doctors shifted the babies to another hospital as smoke billowed into the casualty ward and the first floor, fire officials said

title: Abrogation of Article 370 was the biggest policy mistake by the government: Omar Abdullah
content: Jammu & Kashmir CM demands statehood and “a timeline and parameters on which it will be decided; he says ‘One Nation One Election’ difficult to implement in a “country as diverse as ours”

title: BJP won’t succeed in its ultimate objective, says Congress amid Shiv Sena (UBT) developments
content: The Home Minister is running a wholly cynical campaign that is well lubricated and, like in the mutual funds industry, offers various schemes and products suited to individual needs. There are absolutely no limits to his depravity, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: West Bengal CM launches cleanliness drive at Hooghly river ghat; stresses on 'Namami Gange' initiative
content: BJP leaders Agnimitra Paul and Tapas Roy, along with senior officials of the Kolkata Municipal Corporation, were present on the occasion

title: Bus crashes into truck on highway in Vadodara, killing six, leaving 20 injured
content: The luxury bus, heading from Rajasthan to Surat in Gujarat, crashed into the rear side of the truck, Vadodara District SP Sushil Agrawal said

title: BJP govt. in State will be of nationalists, says CM Suvendu during maiden visit to north Bengal
content: BJP government will work towards giving north Bengal its developmental dues to erase the deprivation meted out to the region earlier, says Suvendu Adhikari during his maiden visit to the region

title: Pursue all legal remedies to protect 50% in-service quota: TNMOA
content: TNMOA urged the State government to protect the in-service quota and to initiate appropriate legal proceedings, including appeals and fresh writ petitions, wherever necessary

title: India-China relations have improved from ‘reset and fresh start’ to ‘new level’: Chinese envoy Xu Feihong
content: Chinese Ambassador to India Xu Feihong in conversation with Suhasini Haidar during the session ‘India-China tango: Asian powers shaping the emerging world order’

title: Body of custodial torture victim Akash Delison taken for cremation following court order
content: He died on March 8 reportedly due to custodial torture by the Manamadurai police; his family refused to receive his body

title: West Bengal CM Adhikari holds Janata Darbar, listens to people’s grievances
content: The first 'janata darbar' was held on May 18. Mr. Adhikari had announced the weekly programme after taking charge as Chief Minister, saying citizens would be able to meet him directly every week

title: VACB’s sting operation uncovers widespread illegal diversion of food grains from NFSA godowns, ration shops in Kerala
content: As part of undercover operation titled Operation Food Safety, anti-corruption sleuths set up staged scenarios, posing as black marketeers seeking to purchase subsidised food grains from randomly chosen National Food Security Act (NFSA) warehouses and ration shops at at prices significantly below market rates

title: Compensatory afforestation for biodiversity loss in Nicobar a bogus argument: Jairam Ramesh
content: ‘In India, unlike in many other parts of the world, when we protect nature, you also protect culture’

title: Lokesh, Pemmasani lay foundation for ₹142-crore Nidamarru railway overbridge in Mangalagiri
content: The four-lane bridge at level crossing gate No. 14 aims to end daily traffic disruptions between Mangalagiri, Amaravati and Tadikonda

title: Character AI poses unique risks for young users, mental health experts warn
content: Excessive dependence on such tools can have unintended consequences for both mental well-being and cognitive functioning

title: Sena (UBT) rebellion speculation: BJP has no role in it, says Bawankule
content: What connection does the BJP have with this? We have no connection. We do not know what 'Operation Tiger' is, who brought it, and why it was brought; we do not know that either. The BJP has no connection with it, says Maharashtra BJP leader Chandrashekhar Bawankule

title: World suffers from shortage of trust: PM Modi to G7 leaders
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump greet each other against a backdrop of strained India-U.S. ties; the two leaders are scheduled to meet on Wednesday

title: After China says no, exporters urge Andhra Pradesh to curb high-risk pesticides in chilli
content: The Chillies Exporters Association sought a ban on Acephate and Methamidophos, warning that residue violations threaten India’s chilli exports to China

title: More than 30 illegal structures demolished as JDA reclaims prime land in Jammu
content: The Jammu Development Authority vice-chairman Rupesh Kumar said public land is a public trust and must be safeguarded from illegal occupation

title: Industry leaders reflect on leadership in times of chaos, disruption and volatility
content: The Hindu Huddle’s session on ‘The Architecture of Leadership: Designing blueprints for a volatile world’

title: The Hindu Huddle | Manoj Bajpayee wants an image makeover
content: You cannot recreate human experience; the real-time performance is always authentic when compared to AI, says the actor in a light-hearted chat with Rohit Khilnani

title: Nara Lokesh asks TDP leaders to hold 45-day campaign on government’s achievements
content: The TDP working president said BJP and Jana Sena leaders would join the June 25 to August 10 drive, and asked cadre to stay alert on the electoral-roll revision

title: T. Veena, daughter of ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, appears before ED for questioning in CMRL pay-off case
content: This is the first time Veena is appearing before ED at its office in the probe linked to allegations that CMRL made payments amounting to ₹2.78 crore to her now-defunct IT firm Exalogic without receiving any services in return

title: Madras High Court sets aside order appointing interim administrator for Chettinad Vidyashram school
content: Division Bench requests a single judge to rehear the issue and pass fresh orders after affording an opportunity to all the parties to file their counter affidavits

title: Major IPS reshuffle in Kerala: IGs, DIGs and SPs shifted in key police posts
content: Putta Vimaladitya, Inspector General of Police, Internal Security, is transferred and posted as Inspector General of Police, North Zone

title: India’s reservoirs can host 102 GW of floating solar, says first national assessment
content: The report states that ground-mounted solar systems, which dominate India’s roughly 100 GW of installed solar capacity, require three to four times more area per megawatt than the panels themselves occupy

title: Supreme Court seeks Centre, States’ response on plea over ‘misuse’ of Aadhaar
content: The petition claims that ‘infiltrators and illegal immigrants’ are able to obtain Aadhaar cards and project themselves as ‘lawful residents’

title: Telegram CEO Durov’s allegation on Reliance role in access disruption needs evidence
content: To understand the significance of the allegation, it is important to understand how the internet works

title: Agencies grill Abhishek Banerjee for third consecutive day
content: The Trinamool national general secretary is questioned in connection with an FIR lodged at the Bidhannagar police station for allegedly making provocative remarks in some speeches posted on social media

title: Mamata visited hills like a ‘tourist’, paid no heed to development, says Suvendu
content: BJP leader promises withdrawal of all ‘false cases’ lodged against Gorkha people by the previous government, revival of 25 tea gardens in north Bengal with ₹330-crore package under the ‘PM Cha Samriddhi Yojana’

title: 3 injured Kuki youths shifted from RIMS in Manipur amid tight security
content: On Tuesday (June 16) night, security forces foiled attempts made by protesters to storm the hospital premises. The demonstrators were demanding that the three youths be handed over to them

title: Tata says India pollution board drops scrutiny of Apple iPhone parts plant
content: Tamil Nadu’s pollution control authority had warned Tata of a forced shutdown unless it explained why government inspections found that wastewater discharge ‌had contaminated open wells in adjacent agricultural lands

title: Is Lok Sabha Speaker opposed to Rahul Gandhi’s Kota visit, asks Ashok Gehlot
content: He said the functionaries linked to the office of Lok Sabha Speaker were pressuring coaching institutes, paying guest accommodations and guesthouse operators to discourage students from attending the programme

title: Madras HC directs State to conduct last rites for Sivaganga custodial torture victim Akash Delison
content: The Bench said the refusal of the family to receive the body leaves the court with no alternative except to authorise the State authorities to proceed in accordance with law

title: Madras High Court dismisses plea to order CBI probe into resignation of four MLAs
content: Chief Justice’s Bench says no such order could be passed in the absence of foundational materials to suspect corrupt practices behind the resignations

title: UP, Gujarat, Jharkhand, 10 other States record revenue surplus in FY25: CAG report
content: Of the 18 States that targeted revenue surplus, nine achieved the target, while Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Mizoram, and Telangana ended up being revenue deficit in 2024-25.

title: India’s rainfall deficit at 35% as monsoon progress stalls
content: Central India faces 63% deficit as monsoon’s first round loses momentum; govt. directs crop-wise contingency plans, puts around 200 districts under priority monitoring, orders weekly reviews

title: TVK government’s White Paper puts Tamil Nadu’s debt at ₹13.18 lakh crore
content: The report says the headline debt figure of ₹10 lakh crore in 2025-26 captures only the State’s direct borrowings through market loans, institutional debt, and public account liabilities; it does not capture the borrowings of the PSUs, statutory boards, and special purpose vehicles that the State guarantees or implicitly supports

title: PM Modi holds bilateral talks with leaders of U.K., UAE on the sidelines of G7 Summit
content: The Modi-Starmer meeting came at a time when the U.K. Prime Minister’s political future is in question – a byelection could put a potential contender for his post, Andy Burnham, back in the U.K. Parliament.

title: Inside Mangav, a unique Maharashtra village for abandoned women and their children
content: Founded by Dr Rajendra and Dr Sucheta Dhamane, Mangav is home to 477 rescued women and 43 children, where residents run a bakery, manage farms and dairy units, and will soon launch a highway café as part of their rehabilitation journey

title: Tamil Nadu’s own-tax effort has ‘collapsed’, says White Paper
content: Giving an account of how T.N.’s own tax revenue fared vis-à-vis 3 ‘peer States’ in last 5 years, the report says the SOTR-to-GSDP ratio declined from 5.93% to 5.45%, the ‘lowest in the State’s history and the steepest decline’ among the benchmarked States

title: Supreme Court steps in to ensure ‘dignified life’ for octogenarian, visually impaired son
content: The top court takes suo motu cognisance of newspaper reports highlighting the dire conditions in which they are living

title: Air Force ferrying NEET question papers by aircraft
content: Bundles flown to more than 20 locations across the country; papers delivered to several northern States, while dry runs were conducted at selected locations across southern India

title: Senior BJP leaders hold meeting, discuss organisational restructuring
content: Party discusses regional in-charges, expansion of office-bearers and organisational changes ahead of new national team and possible Cabinet reshuffle

title: Delhi Police bust Pakistan-backed terror syndicate, seven held
content: Police say the network was functioning under the patronage of Pakistan’s ISI and was using Indians to further terror activities; police have arrested suspects from Uttar Pradesh and Punjab

title: Empty rations and silent radios: the death of an Indian seafarer onboard Celestial Sea
content: The vessel’s problems encompass almost everything that Indian seafarers have faced in the last two-and-a-half months: Depleting supplies, problems with shipowners, distress calls not being attended to, rationing of food and water, long stressful days in the war zone, and the U.S. Navy’s hostile manoeuvres.

title: Over-the-counter sale of cough syrup barred
content: Following the Drugs Rules amendment, lozenges, pills, and tablets for cough will still be available over the counter; 24 children in M.P. died after consuming adulterated cough syrup last year

title: Vijayabaskar quits as MLA, leaves AIADMK
content: He is the fifth AIADMK legislator to resign from the Assembly weeks after the Assembly elections in 2026.

title: Congress Ministers hit back at DMK after facing persistent criticism for joining TVK-led govt.
content: Their joint statement says the DMK must accept the people’s verdict and continue its work as a responsible opposition party

title: 'Rs 50cr offered per MP': Uddhav's Sena on edge as 'Op Tiger' revives memories of 2022 rebellion
content: Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde is reportedly in touch with a group of Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MPs for 'Operation Tiger,' potentially leading to another split. This move, if successful, could significantly weaken Uddhav Thackeray's faction and bolster the NDA's parliamentary strength. Shiv Sena (UBT) leaders have dismissed the reports, dubbing the alleged move 'Operation Wolf'.

title: 'Nitish Kumar has got everything done': Lalu Yadav on withdrawal of Z+ security cover
content: RJD supremo Lalu Prasad Yadav accused Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar of orchestrating the withdrawal of his and Rabri Devi's Z-plus security cover. Following the downgrade, RJD workers took over guarding Rabri Devi's residence, asserting their duty to protect her in the absence of Lalu and Tejashwi Yadav.

title: 'Nothing to do with football': Messi reveals why he cried after Argentina's win
content: Lionel Messi's emotional hat-trick in Argentina's 3-0 World Cup win over Algeria marked a historic night, equaling Miroslav Klose's all-time scoring record.  Despite personal difficulties, Messi delivered a masterful performance, celebrating his 200th international cap with a record-equalling feat.

title: Telegram moves Delhi HC against Centre's ban ahead of NEET-UG re-test
content: Russian messaging platform Telegram has challenged the Centre's decision to ban the app in the Delhi High Court. The temporary ban was imposed ahead of the NEET-UG re-test due to concerns over exam-related content circulation. Telegram is seeking judicial intervention against the government's restriction.

title: US warns Anthropic CEO of penalties over Mythos, Fable 5 ban
content: The US government has instructed AI company Anthropic to ban foreign nationals from accessing its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, citing national security concerns. Secretary of Commerce Howard W. Lutnick sent a letter to Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, stating an unacceptable risk of military-intelligence use or diversion.

title: India may ask refiners to maintain larger crude oil inventories; here's why
content: The disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has challenged a long-standing belief among Indian policymakers that the country's proximity to the crude-rich Persian Gulf reduced the need for large strategic petroleum reserves.

title: FIFA WC: Austria end 36-year wait with dramatic victory over debutants Jordan
content: Austria secured a long-awaited World Cup victory, defeating debutants Jordan 3-1.  Romano Schmid opened the scoring, but Jordan equalised before an own goal and a late Marko Arnautovic penalty sealed the win. This historic triumph sets up a showdown with defending champions Argentina.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: IND vs AFG: Versatile KL Rahul key to India's 2027 ODI World Cup plans
content: The ODI series against Afghanistan serves as an early indicator for India's 2027 World Cup preparations, with KL Rahul emerging as a key middle-order option. His adaptability and ability to accelerate or consolidate innings provide crucial flexibility, making him an important asset regardless of his batting position or wicketkeeping duties.

title: China beats Neuralink in launching what Elon Musk called 'Jesus-level technologies'
content: China has approved the world's first commercial brain-computer chip, NEO, ahead of Elon Musk's Neuralink. This coin-sized device, designed for spinal cord injury and paralysis patients, uses a less invasive approach. NEO has cleared clinical trials and is entering mass production, marking a significant step in brain-computer interface commercialization.

title: Kiran Rao shares a laugh with Aamir Khan's bride-to-be Gauri Spratt during night out
content: Bollywood star Aamir Khan is garnering attention for his modern family dynamics, recently seen with ex-wife Kiran Rao and bride-to-be Gauri Spratt. A viral video captured them sharing a vehicle after a family outing, showcasing a warm rapport between Kiran and Gauri.

title: How US quietly kept Gulf crude moving despite Iran's Hormuz blockade
content: The US military is secretly orchestrating ship-to-ship oil transfers in the Gulf to circumvent Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. This operation, involving smaller tankers transferring oil to larger vessels offshore, aims to keep energy exports flowing despite the crisis. The system relies on compliance checks and monitoring, with millions of barrels moved since early May.

title: Meet Antonela Roccuzzo, Lionel Messi’s childhood love
content: Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo share one of sport's most enduring love stories, rooted in their shared childhood in Rosario, Argentina. They married in July 2017 and have three sons together: Thiago, Mateo, and Ciro. Roccuzzo has built a massive global following in her own right, known less for glamour and more for consistent family presence through every chapter of Messi's extraordinary career. As Messi competes at the 2026 World Cup, their family remains one of the most recognised in football.

title: Will gold prices find support in near-term? Check outlook for today
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are holding strongly and buying from central banks is likely to continue supporting the yellow metal, says Vedika Narvekar, Research Analyst - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: Remote runways, rapid logistics: US Air Force's next airlifter built for Iran-style battlefields
content: The US Air Force is on the lookout for cutting-edge airlift solutions to phase out its aging C-17 and C-5M aircraft. This next-gen aircraft will need to thrive in challenging combat environments, boasting longer range, enhanced durability, and versatile capabilities.

title: Rupee continues to rebound: Currency jumps 31 paise to 94.29 against US dollar
content: The Indian Rupee strengthened significantly against the US dollar on Wednesday, driven by falling crude oil prices and a weaker dollar index. This recovery, fueled by expectations of a US-Iran framework agreement and potential capital inflows, has pushed the rupee towards the 94.00-93.80 zone. Traders are closely watching the upcoming signing of the US-Iran agreement in Geneva.

title: India's defence production more than double in 5 years, hits record Rs 1.78 lakh cr
content: India's defence production reached an all-time high of Rs 1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26, a 15.6% increase from the previous fiscal, underscoring the nation's push for self-reliance. This surge, nearly quadrupling in a decade, reflects significant growth in domestic manufacturing and a record Rs 7.85 lakh crore defence budget allocation.

title: UGC NET June exam admit card released: Check direct link to download hall tickets here
content: The National Testing Agency has issued admit cards for the UGC NET June 2026 examination scheduled across six days later this month. Candidates can access their hall tickets online and are advised to verify all details carefully. The agency has also provided support channels for resolving admit card-related issues and urged aspirants to stay updated through the official website.

title: Messi scripts history with first World Cup hat-trick, equals Klose's goals record
content: Lionel Messi achieved a career milestone, scoring his first World Cup hat-trick to lead Argentina to a 3-0 win over Algeria. This remarkable feat drew him level with Miroslav Klose atop the tournament's all-time scoring charts with 16 goals. The performance also marked his 200th appearance for Argentina and extended his scoring streak to five consecutive World Cup matches.

title: Sooryavanshi survives on 0 amid controversial call, Afghan players left in disbelief
content: India A opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi received a controversial reprieve on nought during a match against Afghanistan A. The third umpire ruled a one-handed catch not out, sparking debate. This incident follows a recent on-field altercation, adding to the young batter's eventful series despite modest scores.

title: Robbery, chase, four bullets: How Pak police's 'mistaken assessment' killed an Australian girl
content: A family visit to Pakistan turned tragic when nine-year-old Australian girl Hania Ahmed was killed in a police shooting. Confronted by robbers, the family's attempt to flee was met with gunfire from Punjab Police's crime control unit. Hania died from bullet wounds, while her father and brother were also injured.

title: Beautiful game, ugly WC: Several teams face brunt of US paranoia, oppressive security
content: Iran's football team faced immense logistical challenges and intrusive security measures during their World Cup campaign in the US, drawing 2-2 against New Zealand.  Players expressed frustration over the "disaster" of unfair competition due to these issues.  Despite political tensions and fan disruptions, the team received support, while Mexico was praised for its hospitality.

title: How Qatar, not Pakistan, became the real power broker in US-Iran deal
content: While Pakistan sought the spotlight in its diplomatic endeavours, it was Qatar’s subtle, low-key mediation that ultimately held the key to the US-Iran ceasefire. Leveraging its unique relationship with Tehran and access to the American administration, Qatar's patient and discreet shuttle diplomacy triumphed over more boisterous efforts that were ridiculed globally.

title: Who pays for repairs in a rented home? What tenants and landlords need to know
content: India's Model Tenancy Act, 2021 clarifies landlord and tenant responsibilities for property upkeep. Landlords are accountable for major repairs, with tenants able to deduct costs from rent if obligations are unmet. Tenants must prevent damage and report issues promptly, facing deductions from their security deposit for failures.

title: Sooryavanshi controversy: Halambage faces sanctions after Dambulla altercation
content: Sri Lanka Cricket has reportedly sanctioned Sri Lanka A spinner Vishen Halambage following the heated post-match altercation with India A batter Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after the Super Over thriller in Dambulla. Wicketkeeper Niroshan Dickwella was also fined for a separate offence. India spin coach Sairaj Bahutule backed the 15-year-old Sooryavanshi, saying the youngster would learn from the incident.

title: Meet Pawan Kumar Chandana: Ex-Isro scientist who now owns India’s largest rocket factory
content: From scoring 51 in Maths to founding India's largest private rocket factory, Pawan Kumar Chandana's journey is inspiring. After a stint at ISRO, he co-founded Skyroot Aerospace, launching India's first privately built rocket. Now valued at $1.1 billion, the company is set to achieve orbital launches, placing India among global space leaders.

title: From passengers to parcels: Noida International Airport to see first cargo flight today
content: Noida International Airport (NIA) is set to handle its first cargo flight, a domestic departure, just days after commencing passenger operations. This marks a significant step in its development as a key aviation and cargo hub for North India, boasting advanced digital infrastructure and substantial handling capacity designed for efficient operations.

title: 'Martial arts don't make women less feminine,' says Kalaripayattu practitioner, Amrutha
content: Amrutha ET, a third-generation Kalaripayattu practitioner, shares how this ancient martial art, initiated by her grandfather, shaped her into a resilient and compassionate individual. She emphasizes that Kalaripayattu is a holistic discipline, fostering mental strength and a balance of warrior spirit and nurturing qualities, challenging misconceptions about women in martial arts.

title: Harsh Dubey's wrong turn and the long road to Team India
content: Growing up in Nagpur, Harsh Dubey wanted to become a batter. While buying books for the fifth grade, he took his father to Ruby Cricket Club in Nagpur, where it all began for him under the watchful eyes of Sanjog Binkar. Later, Madhav Bakre honed his craft at the Nagpur Cricket Academy. But the real turnaround came before his record-breaking 2024-25 domestic season, when he spent three weeks in Chennai with Ramji Srinivasan, the former Head Strength and Conditioning Coach of the Indian cricket team.

title: Inside Trump’s Iran deal: The billions that could soon flow to Tehran
content: A secret US-Iran deal sparks debate over potential billions for Tehran. While officials stress 'pay for performance,' critics draw parallels to the 2015 nuclear accord. A proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund, financed by private investors, hinges on Iran's compliance with nuclear and security commitments.

title: Lionel Messi scripts history, becomes only second player to score in five World Cups
content: Lionel Messi etched his name in history, becoming the second player ever to score in five World Cups. His hat-trick against Algeria secured a dominant lead for Argentina, propelling him to the top of the all-time World Cup scoring charts alongside Miroslav Klose. This remarkable achievement marks another milestone in Messi's illustrious career.

title: Namo Cities, Metro Corridors: Inside Ambitious NCR Regional Plan 2041
content: The NCR Planning Board decided to retain the existing boundaries of the National Capital Region while focusing on more balanced regional growth.

title: Class 12 Topper Dies By Suicide In Dehradun, Leaves "I Love You Mom, Dad" Note
content: Riya Kumari Thapa, a daughter of a 1999 Kargil War veteran, was found dead at her home when she did not open her room on Tuesday morning.

title: Shashi Tharoor's 'Quad' Concern As US Drops "Indo" From Pacific Command Name
content: Over the years, the command emerged as an important institutional channel for India to expand defence cooperation with the US.

title: Opinion: Public Assets, Private Capital: India's Next Reform Step
content: That gap between what India's sovereign assets are worth and what they currently earn is central to India's location in the low-middle income bracket.

content: In the span of just a few months, Irene Roggero Ugues watched her daughter Rossella's behaviour change as social media

title: Opinion: Haunted Or Hunted? Why The Ghosts Of 2015 Continue To Shadow Sukhbir Badal
content: Sukhbir Singh Badal may have completed the religious punishment imposed upon him, but his party is still being made to serve a political punishment.

title: NDTV Tops Reuters Institute Digital News Report India Weekly Reach Rankings For 6th Year In A Row
content: This marks the sixth consecutive year that NDTV has topped the India weekly reach rankings in the Reuters Institute Digital News Report

title: Video: Plane Crashes On Texas Highway, Cockpit Window Broken For Rescue
content: Police say six people were onboard and one was killed. The plane crashed in Laredo shortly after 10 pm.

title: Phones Off, Uddhav Sena MPs Not Reachable, Focus Now Shifts To Delhi
content: Uddhav Thackeray and other senior party leaders are making personal efforts to persuade the MPs. Some parliamentarians have been described as "not reachable".

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 300 Points As Oil Falls To A Three-Month-Low
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent Oil fell another 4.4% to $79.53. Oil prices are down 16% from the high made 5 days ago.

title: Shiv Sena Crisis Live: Uddhav Camp Calls Party Meet Tomorrow, Issues Notice To Rebel MPs
content: Uddhav Thackeray Shiv Sena Crisis Live: Shiv Sena (Uddhav camp) MP Sanjay Raut alleged that Rs 15 crore was being paid individually to "buy MPs".

title: Indian Firms Facing Big Talent Shock As 73% Women Quit Jobs After Childbirth
content: India has made notable progress in strengthening maternity benefits. But workplace infrastructure has not evolved at the same pace, say experts.

title: US-Iran LIVE Updates: US-Iran Deal To Be Signed In Switzerland On Friday
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: No Nukes, $300 Billion Fund: What's Inside US-Iran 14-Point Deal
content: The reported text shows that the terms agreed upon between Washington and Tehran have no concrete solution regarding Iran's nuclear programme apart from a pledge to never produce or obtain a nuclear weapon and openness to further discuss other detail

title: Elon Musk's AI Tool Grok Was Used In Strikes Against Iran: US
content: The US military's transition to AI is taking time, and in March the government had to acknowledge that Claude was being used for the war in Iran.

title: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: PM Modi, Trump To Hold Bilateral To Discuss Trade, AI, Global Security
content: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: This is India's 13th participation in the G7 as a guest nation and PM Modi's seventh consecutive appearance at the summit.

title: 'Decline recognition to separate faction': Shiv Sena (UBT) MP writes to LS Speaker
content: the claim that Sena represents the ‘real Shiv Sena’ is sub judice in the Supreme Court, and this letter is being written without prejudice to that claim . there were reports that 6 Sena (UBT) MPs reached Delhi early on Wednesday . it was speculation they could form a separate group by giving a letter to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla .

title: Day after US, Iran agreed to tentative deal, Israeli strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
content: the tentative deal to end the war with the US would require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon . a US official has said the deal did not call for an Israeli withdrawal . on Tuesday, Israeli drone strikes in leabanon killed at least four people .

title: Texas plane crash: Video captures rescuers trying to free people through cockpit window
